# Extracao: transcription_mp4_3__Calling

- **Fonte:** transcription_mp4_3__Calling.txt
- **Tipo:** TXT

---

Phone calls, calling people, talking to them is one of my preferred ways. Why? Because I get to talk to them. It's scary. I'm always scared when I call somebody. Are they going to answer? Are they going to send me to voicemail? Are they going to deny me? Are they going to reject me? Are they going to tell me not to call back? Am I being too persistent? I go through all those same things too. But when I call somebody, I know I'm talking to somebody. I get to go at home at night and I get to look my two little girls in the face and say, I called a lot of people. But Papa, did you tell anything? No, but I talk to 29 people and I know this. If I talk to enough people every day, I'm going to sell something. So where do you call? When do you call? Who do you call? Office, home, cell. Which one's first? Cell first. Office second. Third is home. First is cell, then the office, then home. Who do I call? Who do I talk to? I talk to everybody. When I make one phone call, I could turn it into six or eight phone calls. When I call a company and ask for Bob and Bob's not there, first thing I do is say, can I go to Bob's voicemail? I leave a voicemail for Bob and then I hit zero on the way back. I get to sit back to the receptionist. Who's underneath Bob? That'd be Jack. Good, give me Jack. Jack, we go to voicemail on Jack. Jack, Grant Cardone, I just left a message for Bob. Wanted to leave a message for you. Give me a call. Here's my direct number. I'm going to give a private number out to both Bob and Jack and I'm going to hit zero again, go back to the receptionist. She'll pick it up and I'll be like, who's underneath Jack? That's kind of follow up. I'm talking about, I'm talking about crazy. I'm talking about cray cray follow up, okay? I go back to the receptionist. Who's underneath Bob? Who's underneath Jack? Who's underneath Jack? Shelly's underneath Jack. Good, give me Shelly. Call Shelly. Shelly picks up the phone. See, I finally talked to somebody. That's the first call I've made today. Talking to somebody, not leaving a message. Always leave a message and always talk to somebody. If I got to go to the grunt, the person that opens the doors and they put in the back and they keep away from everyone, if that's the person I have to talk to, I am going to talk to somebody, okay? The cell number as I spoke of earlier is the holy grail of follow up. You want it, they have it, it's in their pocket, it never leaves them. When you make a phone call, have a purpose. The reason I'm calling, your call should start like this. The reason I am calling, the intention of this call, you need a specific reason. Also, keep it tight. I don't have all day. I don't want to do pleasantries with everyone. When you call me to sell me something, grant, I know you're busy, I'll keep it tight. I need four minutes to explain a project I'm on. First, how are you doing? Excellent, pleasantries out of the way. I got three minutes and 50 seconds left. Let me tell you what I'm up to and how you could help me. Keep it tight and then set it up for the next call if it didn't go any place. Always leave a voice message. Home, no, sell, yes, office, always. And then just go all the way down the line up. Use the phone, use it often, make sure you make contact.
